FairPlayCard.title: Advanced: Fair Play
FairPlayCard.noAvg: No one has started playing yet, so there's no average for now
FairPlayCard.fairDesc: Blocks players who've played a lot so others get a turn
FairPlayCard.graceHint: No limit until they've played this many
FairPlayCard.notEnforcedHint: Not enough players in rotation (need ≥ 5) or just saved — no one is blocked for now; it kicks in once more join.
FairPlayCard.autoAdjustHint: The number adjusts automatically as everyone plays more; blocked players are restored once others catch up.
FairPlayCard.autoShowGames: "Show games" was turned on automatically (Fair Play needs everyone to see game counts to be fair).
FairPlayCard.showGamesDesc: The front-end shows how many games each person has played
FairPlayCard.howItWorks: How does this work? (tap to expand)
FairPlayCard.rule1: Once on, the system calculates the average games of the players who've played the most.
FairPlayCard.rule2d: temporarily can't go on court or join the waitlist
FairPlayCard.rule2e: , so players with fewer games go first.
FairPlayCard.rule3a: Once others catch up and they're no longer over, they're
FairPlayCard.rule3c: — no need to lift it manually.
FairPlayCard.rule4: Players who haven't hit the grace-game count are fully unrestricted (grabbing spots by skill early on is fine).
FairPlayCard.rule5: Disabled when fewer than 5 are in rotation, to avoid leaving courts empty.
FairPlayCard.rule6: Best turned on for the final stretch when you want things more even; leave it off otherwise.
